title: ConstructionPlaneInput Object
description: A ConstructionPlaneInput is a throwaway object used to create a ConstructionPlane. The usage pattern is: a. create a ConstructionPlaneInput (ConstructionPlanes.CreateInput) b. call one of the member functions to specify how the ConstructionPlane is created c. create the ConstructionPlane (call ConstructionPlanes.Add) d. stop referencing the ConstructionPlaneInput (so it gets deleted).

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setByAngle
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through an edge, axis or line at a specified angle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByAngleOnCurvedFace
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through the axis inferred from a cylindrical or conical curved face at a specified angle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByDistanceOnPath
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane normal to, and at specified distance along, a path defined by an edge or sketch profile. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByOffset
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane that is offset from a planar face or construction plane at a specified distance. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByOffsetThroughPoint
methods.description: Defines a construction plane that is offset from a planar face or construction plane and whose offset distance is defined by a vertex, sketch point, or construction point where the plane passes through the point.

methods.name: setByPlane
methods.description: This input method is for creating a non-parametric construction plane positioned in space as defined by the input Plane object.   This method of defining a construction plane is only valid when working in the direct modeling mode. This is not valid when working in the parametric modeling mode and will fail.

methods.name: setByTangent
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane tangent to a cylindrical or conical face at a specified point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByTangentAtPoint
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane tangent to a face and aligned to a point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByThreePoints
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through three points that define a triangle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByTwoEdges
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane that passes through two coplanar linear entities or axes. Defines a plane by specifying two coplanar linear entities. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

methods.name: setByTwoPlanes
methods.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane at the midpoint between two planar faces or construction planes. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.

properties.name: creationOccurrence
properties.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the ConstructionPlane is created based on geometry (e.g. a planarEntity) in another component AND (the ConstructionPlane) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: targetBaseOrFormFeature
properties.description: When creating a construction plane that is owned by a base or form feature, set this property to the base or form feature you want to associate the new construction plane with. By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base or form feature.   Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a construction plane associated with a base or form feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base or form feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base or form feature. The base or form feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

classType.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setByAngle.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByAngle Method
setByAngle.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through an edge, axis or line at a specified angle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByAngle.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful

parameters.name: linearEntity
parameters.description: The axis about which to rotate the plane

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The angle at which to create the plane

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar face or construction plane the angle is measured from.

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByDistanceOnPath.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByDistanceOnPath Method
setByDistanceOnPath.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane normal to, and at specified distance along, a path defined by an edge or sketch profile. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByDistanceOnPath.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful.

parameters.name: pathEntity
parameters.description: The path can be an edge, sketch curve, or a path of multiple entities.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: The distance is a value from 0 to 1 indicating the position along the path where 0 is at the start and 1 is at the end.

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByOffset.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByOffset Method
setByOffset.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane that is offset from a planar face or construction plane at a specified distance. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByOffset.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A plane, planar face or construction plane from which to create the offset plane

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: ValueInput object that specifies the offset distance for the plane

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

related_samples.name: Loft Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new loft feature.

setByOffsetThroughPoint.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByOffsetThroughPoint Method
setByOffsetThroughPoint.description: Defines a construction plane that is offset from a planar face or construction plane and whose offset distance is defined by a vertex, sketch point, or construction point where the plane passes through the point.
setByOffsetThroughPoint.return_description: Returns true if construction plane definition is successful.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: A planar BRepFace, ConstructionPlane, or Plane object that the new construction plane will be offset from. A Plane object is only valid in a direct-edit design or a base feature. In that case a non-parametric result is created.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: A BRepVertex, SketchPoint, ConstructionPoint, or Point3D that defines the offset distance. A Point3D is only valid in a direct-edit design. In that case a non-parametric result is created.

setByPlane.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByPlane Method
setByPlane.description: This input method is for creating a non-parametric construction plane positioned in space as defined by the input Plane object. This method of defining a construction plane is only valid when working in the direct modeling mode. This is not valid when working in the parametric modeling mode and will fail.
setByPlane.return_description: Returns true if the construction plane definition is successful.

parameters.name: plane
parameters.description: A transient plane object

setByTangent.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByTangent Method
setByTangent.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane tangent to a cylindrical or conical face at a specified point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTangent.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful

parameters.name: tangentFace
parameters.description: A cylindrical or conical face to create the plane tangent to

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The angle relative to the planarEntity input at which to create the tangent plane

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar face or construction plane the tangent is measured from.

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByTangentAtPoint.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByTangentAtPoint Method
setByTangentAtPoint.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane tangent to a face and aligned to a point. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTangentAtPoint.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful.

parameters.name: tangentFace
parameters.description: A face to create the plane tangent to

parameters.name: pointEntity
parameters.description: A construction point, sketch point or vertex the tangent plane aligns to. This point need not lie on the tangent face.

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByThreePoints.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByThreePoints Method
setByThreePoints.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through three points that define a triangle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByThreePoints.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful. This will fail if the points do not form a triangle (no two points can be coincident and all three cannot be colinear).

parameters.name: pointEntityOne
parameters.description: The first construction point, sketch point or vertex in the triangle

parameters.name: pointEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second construction point, sketch point or vertex in the triangle

parameters.name: pointEntityThree
parameters.description: The third construction point, sketch point or vertex in the triangle

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByTwoEdges.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByTwoEdges Method
setByTwoEdges.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane that passes through two coplanar linear entities or axes. Defines a plane by specifying two coplanar linear entities. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTwoEdges.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful. This will fail if the two linear entities are not coplanar.

parameters.name: linearEntityOne
parameters.description: The first of two coplanar linear entities to define the plane

parameters.name: linearEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second of two coplanar linear entities to define the plane

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByTwoPlanes.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByTwoPlanes Method
setByTwoPlanes.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane at the midpoint between two planar faces or construction planes. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByTwoPlanes.return_description: Returns true if the creation of the ConstructionPlaneInput is successful. This will fail if the two planes are co-planar.

parameters.name: planarEntityOne
parameters.description: The first planar face or construction plane to create a bisecting plane between

parameters.name: planarEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second planar face or construction plane to create a bisecting plane between

related_samples.name: Construction Plane API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating construction plane by different ways.

setByAngleOnCurvedFace.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.setByAngleOnCurvedFace Method
setByAngleOnCurvedFace.description: This input method is for creating a construction plane through the axis inferred from a cylindrical or conical curved face at a specified angle. This can result in a parametric or non-parametric construction plane depending on whether the parent component is parametric or is a direct edit component.
setByAngleOnCurvedFace.return_description: Returns true if the construction plane definition is successful.

parameters.name: curvedFace
parameters.description: The cylindrical or conical curved face from which the axis of rotation is inferred.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: The angle at which to create the plane.

parameters.name: planarEntity
parameters.description: The planar face or construction plane the angle is measured from. A zero angle creates a plane whose normal is aligned with this plane's normal, so the result is deterministic when an explicit planarEntity is provided.

creationOccurrence.title: ConstructionPlaneInput.creationOccurrence Property
creationOccurrence.description: In order for geometry to be transformed correctly, an Occurrence for creation needs to be specified when the ConstructionPlane is created based on geometry (e.g. a planarEntity) in another component AND (the ConstructionPlane) is not in the root component. The CreationOccurrence is analogous to the active occurrence in the UI

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = constructionPlaneInput_var.creationOccurrence

# Set the value of the property.
constructionPlaneInput_var.creationOccurrence = propertyValue

syntax.python:

By default, this is null, meaning it will not be associated with a base or form feature. Because of a current limitation, if you want to create a construction plane associated with a base or form feature, you must set this property AND call the startEdit method of the base or form feature, create the feature, and then call the finishEdit method of the base or form feature. The base or form feature must be in an "edit" state to be able to add any additional items to it.
